sourceMovie.title: Super
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Super (2010) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Defendor
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Defendor (2009) operates in the same comedy as Super, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The funny payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Deadpool
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Deadpool (2016) belongs to the same conversation as Super — both anchor their comedy in action emotion that doesn't let go. The funny undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: The Lobster
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Super's comedy stayed with you, The Lobster (2015) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The drama through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: 2 Guns
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Super excels in comedy, 2 Guns (2013) doubles down on action to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Hitman's Wife's Bodyguard
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Hitman's Wife's Bodyguard (2021) is the natural next step after Super: same comedy DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and action elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Shoot 'Em Up
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Super who respond to its comedy will find Shoot 'Em Up (2007) immediately familiar. Both films share a action sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: The Naked Gun
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Super fans keep landing on The Naked Gun (2025) — the comedy is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Kick-Ass
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Kick-Ass draws from the same action well as Super, channeling crime in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2010 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The Mask
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Mask (1994) captures the comedy appeal of Super without simply imitating it. The romance streak running through both films is what puts The Mask at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Kick-Ass 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of Super — that tense edge, the adventure — translates cleanly into Kick-Ass 2 (2013). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

faq.question: What should I watch after Super?
faq.answer: Try Defendor, Deadpool, The Lobster—each shares DNA with Super while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Super?
faq.answer: Super (2010) is best described as Comedy, Action, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Super worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
